title: Gas inflatable balloons
abstract: A system and method for simultaneously inflating a plurality of balloons with pressurised gas issuing from a pressurised gas supply, each balloon being releasably or permanently attached to an inflation conduit which serves to duct gas from the gas supply to its respective balloon, and each balloon having an associated valve to control the release of gas from inside the balloon when connected and/or disconnected from its associated balloon.
claims:
Claims 1. A balloon for use in a system for inflating balloons with pressurised gas issuing from a pressurised gas supply, said balloon comprising an inflatable body with a neck region defining an opening of the inflatable body through which pressurised gas can pass to inflate the balloon, and wherein said balloon is connectable to an inflation conduit that is adapted to duct pressurised gas from the pressurised gas supply to said inflatable body, and wherein said balloon comprises a valve configurable between an open condition to permit the entry of gas to the inflatable body via the inflation conduit and a closed condition to restrict the egress of gas from said inflatable body via the inflation conduit, said valve being located inside the inflatable body, and wherein at or adjacent the opening at least a first portion of an interior surface of the neck region is bonded to itself forming at least one bonded region to the seal the opening, save for a passage leading from the opening into the neck region. 2. The balloon of claim 1 wherein the opening is sealed with a single bonded region, save for the passage leading from the opening into the neck region. 3. The balloon of claim 1 wherein a portion of an interior surface of the neck region is bonded to itself forming a first bonded region, and wherein a further portion of the interior surface of the neck region is bonded to itself forming a second bonded region, and wherein the first and second bonded regions together seal the opening, save for the passage leading from the opening into the neck region, the first and second bonded regions being located on either side of the passage. 4. The balloon of any one of claims 1 to 3 wherein the size of the opening is significantly larger than the width of the passage. 5. The balloon of claim 3 wherein the width of the passage is less than 1/2 of the width of the opening. 6. The balloon of claim 3 wherein the width of the passage is less than 1/4 of the width of the opening. 7. The balloon of claim 1 wherein the bonded region(s) is/are formed by any one or more of applying an adhesive, lamination, and heat welding. 8. The balloon of claim 2 wherein the bonded region(s) comprise(s) adhesive applied to engage interior surfaces of the neck such that the adhesive is not visible at the exterior of the balloon. 9. The balloon of claim 2 or claim 3 wherein the bonded region(s) comprise(s) adhesive applied in a flowable state, which adhesive forms a bead across the opening. 10. The balloon of any one of claims 1 to 9 wherein the bonded region(s) has/have a flat profile. 11. The balloon of any one of claims 1 to 10 wherein at the bonded region(s) said interior surface of the neck region is bonded directly onto itself. 12. The balloon of any one of claims 1 to 4 wherein said valve is located at the opening of the inflatable body, inside of the neck region, and bonded to the neck region at at least one of said bonded regions. 13. The balloon of any one of claims 1 to 12 wherein the bonded region(s) has/have been formed by pressing or pinching the neck region with the valve in situ. 14. The balloon of any one of claims 1 to 13 wherein said valve is a one-way valve. 15. The balloon of claim 14 wherein said valve is a one-way valve that adopts a substantially flat profile in the closed condition. 16. The balloon of claim 13 or claim 14 wherein the valve is duckbill valve comprising two plies of flexible sheet material joined with one another in a manner to define a sealable passage therethrough. 17. The balloon of claim 16 wherein each of the plies are made of a flexible sheet material less than 1mm in thickness. 18. The balloon of claim 17 wherein the plies of the valve are made from one or more flexible sheet materials selected from polyurethane, polyester, polypropylene, and PVC. 19. The balloon of any one of claims 16 to 18 wherein said inflation conduit comprises a first end and a second end, and wherein said sealable passage comprises a first end and a second end, and wherein said first end of said inflation conduit can be inserted into the first end of said sealable passage with the plies of the valve sealed around its exterior, and wherein the sealable passage of the valve is able to collapse at its second end so as to seal the passage about the first end of the inflation conduit. 20. The balloon of any one of claims 1 to 19 wherein the valve includes a connector to releasably connect with the inflation conduit. 21. The balloon of claim 20 wherein the connector is a rigid part. 22. The balloon of claim 20 or 21 wherein the connector is a tubular piece configured to releasably connect with the inflation conduit by engaging a first end of the inflation conduit in a friction fit. 23. The balloon of any one of claims 20 to claim 22 wherein the connector is adapted to releasably engage with the first end of the inflation conduit at an engagement region, 

 and wherein the connector is located at least partially inside the sealable passage of the valve with the engagement region projecting out of a first end of said passage, and wherein the plies of the valve are sealed around the exterior of the connector, and wherein the sealable passage of the valve is able to collapse at a second end so as to seal the passage about an end of the connector opposing the engagement region. 24. The balloon of any one of claims 20 to 24 wherein said connector extends through the passage at the neck region of the inflatable body, and wherein at least a second portion of the interior surface of the neck region, said second portion being a portion that defines the passage, is bonded to the connector. 25. The balloon of any one of claims 1 to 24 wherein said balloon has a permanently attached flexible tether of elongate cord or strip form. 26. The balloon of claim 25 where said tether is permanently attached by incorporation into the bonded region at the neck of the balloon. 27. The balloon of claim 25 or claim 26 wherein said tether is provided in a coiled configuration. 28. The balloon of anyone of claims 1 to 27 wherein said inflatable body is made of an elastically expandable material. 29. The balloon of any one of claims 1 to 28 wherein said balloon and valve are configured to permit a user to orally inflate the balloon by blowing air through the opening of the inflatable body either directly or through the inflation conduit to cause expansion of the inflatable body. 30. A balloon assembly for use in a system for inflating balloons with pressurised gas issuing from a pressurised gas supply, comprising a balloon as claimed in any one of claims 1 to 29 and an inflation conduit connected to said balloon. 31. The balloon of any one of claims 1 to 29 wherein said inflation conduit extends through the passage at the neck region of the inflatable body, and wherein at least a second portion of the interior surface of the neck region, said second portion being a portion that defines the passage, is bonded to the inflation conduit. 32. The balloon of claims 31 wherein the bonded region(s) has/have been formed by pressing or pinching the neck region with the inflation conduit in situ. 33. A system for simultaneously inflating a plurality of balloons comprising a plurality of balloon assemblies as claimed in any one of claims 30 to 32, wherein each one of the plurality of balloon assemblies is joined with at least one other balloon assembly by a severable connection between their respective inflation conduits. 34. A method of simultaneously inflating a plurality of balloons with pressurised gas issuing from a pressurised gas supply via a plurality of inflation conduits, 

 each of said inflation conduits being configured to duct pressurised gas from the pressurised gas supply to a respective one of the balloons, wherein said method comprises the steps of: a) providing a plurality of balloons, each in fluid communication with the pressurised gas supply via a respective one of the inflation conduits, and b) simultaneously inflating the balloons with pressurised gas. 35. The method of claim 34 wherein the method further comprises one or more steps selected from: a) connecting the inflation conduits to be in fluid communication with the gas supply, and b) releasing the inflation tubes from fluid communication with the gas supply, and c) re-connecting one or more of the inflation conduits to be in fluid communication with the gas supply. 36. The method of claim 34 or 35 wherein the method further comprises one or more steps selected from: a) connecting the balloons with the inflation conduits, b) disconnecting the balloons from the inflation conduits, and c) re-connecting one or more of the balloons with respective ones of the inflation conduits. 37. The method of any one of claims 34 to 36 wherein the method further comprises the step of tethering or anchoring one of more of the balloons using one or more selected from: a) a tether connected to the balloon, b) an inflation conduit, and c) a connector of the balloon which is adapted to connect to both of the inflation conduit and an corresponding connector of a balloon support surface or frame.
